Weekly Course Schedule

Week 1: Long-Term Care Foundations, Resident Rights, and Professional Accountability

Class week: Jun 24, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 1, 2, and 3

Focus: Long-term care settings, nursing team roles, delegation, resident rights, ethics, laws, abuse prevention, work ethics, and professional behavior.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 1 Discussion: Dignity, Rights, and the Long-Term Care Nurse

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Long-Term Care Role and Resident Rights Scenario - due Jun 28, 2026

Week 2: Communication, Nursing Process, Culture, Aging, and Person-Centered Care

Class week: Jul 1, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10

Focus: Communication with residents and the health team, assisting with the nursing process, understanding the whole resident, cultural and religious considerations, body systems review, aging, and sexuality.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 2 Discussion: Seeing the Whole Resident

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Resident Profile and Communication Plan - due Jul 5, 2026

Week 3: Safety, Falls, Restraint Alternatives, Infection Prevention, and Safe Mobility

Class week: Jul 8, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 11, 12, 13, 14, and 15

Focus: Environmental safety, fall prevention, restraint alternatives, standard precautions, infection prevention, body mechanics, transfers, and safe resident handling.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 3 Discussion: Preventing Harm Before It Happens

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Safety and Infection Prevention Room Audit - due Jul 12, 2026

Week 4: Resident Unit, Bedmaking, Hygiene, Grooming, Nutrition, Fluids, and Supportive Care

Class week: Jul 15, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, and 21

Focus: Resident room safety and comfort, bedmaking, bathing, oral care, grooming, nutrition, hydration, feeding assistance, aspiration precautions, nutritional support, and IV therapy observation limits.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 4 Discussion: Basic Care Is Skilled Care

Assignment: [PN103 2026] ADL, Nutrition, and Hydration Care Plan - due Jul 19, 2026

Week 5: Elimination, Exercise, Activity, Comfort, Rest, Oxygen Needs, and Vital Signs

Class week: Jul 22, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, and 27

Focus: Urinary and bowel elimination, continence support, exercise, activity, comfort, rest, sleep, oxygen needs, respiratory therapies, and vital-sign measurement.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 5 Discussion: Noticing Changes in Condition

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Elimination, Oxygen, and Vital Signs Case Worksheet - due Jul 26, 2026

Week 6: Physical Examination Support, Specimens, Admissions, Transfers, Discharges, and Midterm Review

Class week: Jul 29, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 28, 29, and 30; review Chapters 1-27

Focus: Assisting with examinations, collecting and testing specimens within policy, admission routines, room transfers, discharges, continuity of care, documentation, and midterm synthesis.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 6 Discussion: Smooth Transitions of Care

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Midterm Exam - Long-Term Care Foundations and Daily Care - due Aug 2, 2026

Week 7: Wound Care, Pressure Injuries, Sensory Problems, Cancer, Immune, and Skin Disorders

Class week: Aug 5, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 31, 32, 33, and 34

Focus: Wound observation, nonsterile dressing support within scope, pressure-injury risk, prevention, skin changes, sensory impairment, cancer, immune conditions, and skin disorders.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 7 Discussion: Skin Integrity and Quality of Life

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Skin Integrity and Pressure Injury Prevention Map - due Aug 9, 2026

Week 8: Common Chronic Disorders Across Body Systems

Class week: Aug 12, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 35, 36, 37, and 38

Focus: Nervous, musculoskeletal, cardiovascular, respiratory, digestive, endocrine, urinary, and reproductive disorders commonly affecting long-term care residents.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 8 Discussion: Chronic Conditions and Daily Nursing Priorities

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Chronic Disorder Nursing Priorities Case - due Aug 16, 2026

Week 9: Mental Health, Confusion, Dementia, and Developmental Disabilities

Class week: Aug 19, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 39, 40, and 41

Focus: Mental health disorders, confusion, delirium, dementia, Alzheimer's disease, behavioral expressions, communication strategies, safety, developmental disabilities, and family support.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 9 Discussion: Dementia Care With Dignity

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Dementia and Mental Health Communication Plan - due Aug 23, 2026

Week 10: Rehabilitation, Restorative Nursing, Assisted Living, and Basic Emergency Care

Class week: Aug 26, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapters 42, 43, and 44

Focus: Restorative nursing, rehabilitation goals, functional independence, assisted living service plans, transfers/discharges from assisted living, basic emergency response, stroke, seizure, burns, hemorrhage, shock, and BLS awareness.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 10 Discussion: Restorative Care and Emergency Readiness

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Restorative Nursing and Emergency Response Worksheet - due Aug 30, 2026

Week 11: End-of-Life Care, Family Support, Caregiver Wellness, and Comprehensive Review

Class week: Sep 2, 2026

Reading: Selected readings from Chapter 45; review Chapters 1-44

Focus: Terminal illness, comfort needs, family support, legal issues, signs of death, postmortem care awareness, grief, caregiver wellness, and final review.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 11 Discussion: Comfort, Family, and Dignity at End of Life

Assignment: [PN103 2026] End-of-Life Comfort and Family Support Reflection - due Sep 6, 2026

Week 12: Final Synthesis: Safe, Respectful Long-Term Care Nursing

Class week: Sep 9, 2026

Reading: Comprehensive review of selected course chapters

Focus: Comprehensive synthesis of resident-centered long-term care nursing, safety, communication, chronic illness, dementia, restorative care, end-of-life care, and professional accountability.

Discussion: [PN103 2026] Week 12 Discussion: Professional Commitment to Long-Term Care

Assignment: [PN103 2026] Final Comprehensive Exam - Long-Term Care Nursing - due Sep 9, 2026
